header.title: Canonical Tag Generator — Prevent Duplicate Content Issues
header.description: Free online canonical tag generator to create canonical URL tags that prevent duplicate content issues. Specify the preferred version of any webpage with automatic HTTPS conversion, trailing slash normalization, and query parameter stripping. Essential SEO tool for webmasters and content managers.
header.keywords: canonical tag generator, canonical url generator, canonical tag, duplicate content fix, canonical url, seo canonical tag

about.title: What is a Canonical Tag?
about.desc: A canonical tag tells search engines which URL is the "preferred" version of a page, preventing duplicate content penalties.

mistakes.title: Common Mistakes
mistakes.redirectChain: Canonical pointing to a redirect chain
mistakes.relativeUrls: Using relative URLs instead of absolute
mistakes.inBody: Canonical in the body instead of <head>

single.normalizedFromOriginal: ↑ URL was normalized from the original
single.customCanonicalSub: Override the normalized URL (e.g. cross-domain canonical)

analysisMessages.notHttps: URL is not HTTPS — canonical should use HTTPS
analysisMessages.hasFragment: URL contains a fragment (#) — canonical tags should not include fragments
analysisMessages.considerWww: Consider whether www or non-www is your canonical preference
analysisMessages.trailingSlashConsistency: Trailing slash — be consistent across all canonical URLs
analysisMessages.queryParameters: URL contains query parameters — canonical should usually point to the clean URL

crossDomain.description: Hreflang tags tell Google which language/region version to show users. Every page in the set must reference all other versions, including itself.
crossDomain.xDefaultSub: Points to the fallback version (usually English or the homepage)
crossDomain.outputLabel: Hreflang Tags (add to every page in the set)
crossDomain.importantDesc: These tags must be added to ALL pages in the set. Each page must reference all other language versions, not just the main page.

howToUse.title: How to Use Canonical Tag Generator
howToUse.step1Desc: Paste the page URL you want to generate a canonical tag for
howToUse.step2Desc: Set normalization preferences like HTTPS enforcement, trailing slash handling, and www preference
howToUse.step3Desc: Check the URL analysis for potential issues like query parameters or fragments
howToUse.step4Desc: Copy the generated canonical tag and paste it into the <head> section of your page

items.question: What is a canonical tag?
items.answer: A canonical tag (rel="canonical") is an HTML element that helps prevent duplicate content issues by specifying the preferred version of a webpage to search engines.

items.question: When should I use a canonical tag?
items.answer: Use canonical tags when you have duplicate or similar content across multiple URLs, such as printer-friendly versions, session IDs, or multiple category paths.

items.question: Does a canonical tag guarantee ranking?
items.answer: No, the canonical tag is a hint, not a directive. Search engines may choose a different URL if they believe it better represents the content.

items.question: Can I use canonical tags across domains?
items.answer: Yes, cross-domain canonical tags tell search engines that a piece of content on another domain is the preferred version, useful for syndicated content.
